perturbation.original_text: Fees for the Content shall be $5,000 per year. Additional anatomical line drawings shall be licensed to Emdeon at an incremental charge of $150 per drawing.
perturbation.changed_text: Fees for the Content shall be determined based on market value at the time of renewal. Additional anatomical line drawings shall be licensed to Emdeon, with the price to be determined at the time of request.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Exhibit B removes the fixed fee for the content and additional drawings, introducing uncertainty. While the main agreement still refers to 'amounts set forth on Exhibit B', the values are now undefined. This omission creates a contradiction because Section 4(a) references a now-ambiguous fee structure. Market value is no defined within the text, so we don't know what market value is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration for provision of the Content and all rights granted hereunder, Emdeon hereby agreed to pay WebMD the amounts set forth on Exhibit B of this Agreement. All amounts payable shall be due be due thirty (30) days after the receipt of WebMD's invoice.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified fixed fees for the content and additional drawings. The changed text removes these fixed fees, replacing them with a determination based on 'market value' and a price 'to be determined at the time of request'. The contradicted text states that Emdeon will pay WebMD 'the amounts set forth on Exhibit B'. Since Exhibit B no longer contains specific amounts but instead refers to 'market value' and a price to be determined later, this creates a contradiction. The contradicted section expects defined amounts, but the changed Exhibit B provides an undefined or yet-to-be-determined fee structure. The reference to 'amounts set forth on Exhibit B' in the contradicted text now points to an ambiguous determination method, creating a clear and direct conflict regarding how much Emdeon is obligated to pay and when. This aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction, where essential language is removed or altered in one section, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Emdeon is hereby granted a non-exclusive and worldwide right to use WebMD's trademarks and logos ("Marks") for the purpose of identifying the origin of the Content during the term of this Agreement. These Marks may be used by Emdeon, in its sole discretion, in connection with the Content in the Software, and in any form, format, forum, media, medium, means or method by which the Content is delivered. Notwithstanding the foregoing, portions of the Software that contain Content shall include a WebMD Mark in appearance and format mutually agreed to by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Emdeon is granted the right to mention WebMD as the content source. Details of how to represent WebMD trademarks will be provided separately.
perturbation.explanation: By changing Section 5, the specific right to use WebMD's trademarks and logos ('Marks') is removed and replaced with a vague instruction about mentioning WebMD. This creates a contradiction because it removes the detailed permissions for using the marks but alludes to 'details' to be 'provided separately', which are nowhere to be found in the contract. This makes how Emdeon can promote where the content came from is now vague.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Emdeon is granted the right to mention WebMD as the content source. Details of how to represent WebMD trademarks will be provided separately.
perturbation.justification: The original text granted Emdeon a "non-exclusive and worldwide right to use WebMD's trademarks and logos". The changed text replaces this clear right with a vague instruction to "mention WebMD" and await separate details, which are nowhere to be found. The contradiction lies in the removal of specific trademark usage rights and the introduction of undefined details that are supposed to be provided later. This removal conflicts with the expectation of clear usage rights previously established, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: WebMD shall promptly notify Emdeon when a new Update is available.
perturbation.changed_text: WebMD may, at its discretion, inform Emdeon when new Updates are available.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying section 1(c) of the Terms and Conditions, the obligation for WebMD to promptly notify Emdeon of new content Updates is removed and replaced with a discretionary approach. This change creates uncertainty and potential conflict because the original clause mandates a prompt notification, while the modified clause provides no assurance of timely notification. Now Emdeon can't expect the new updates or they have to somehow find out for themselves.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If WebMD should reprint, issue a new edition, change or modify the edition, or otherwise enhance or edit the Content Emdeon has licensed (collectively, "Updates"), Emdeon has the option, at no additional cost, to receive the most recent Update. WebMD may, at its discretion, inform Emdeon when new Updates are available.
perturbation.justification: The original text created an obligation for WebMD to promptly notify Emdeon of new Updates. The changed text provides that WebMD may, at its discretion, inform Emdeon when new Updates are available. This directly contradicts the idea that Emdeon 'has the option, at no additional cost, to receive the most recent Update' because WebMD is no longer obligated to inform Emdeon of updates, making it unclear if Emdeon would even know when an update is available to receive. The change therefore introduces a contradiction by moving from mandatory notification to discretionary notification.
